payload.title: Unified Engine: marquee-deprecated — <marquee> elements are deprecated
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `marquee-deprecated`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/marquee-deprecated`, `/engine/marquee-deprecated_success`, `/engine/marquee-deprecated_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/marquee-deprecated/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** <marquee> elements are obsolete and should not be used. They are not part of any HTML or WAI-ARIA specification. They are not responsive or supported by any browsers.
**Remediation advice:** Remove the <marquee> element from the page. If you need to display scrolling text, use CSS animations or JavaScript to create a similar effect.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `marquee-deprecated` examples published at `/engine/marquee-deprecated_success` and `/engine/marquee-deprecated_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 1 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

steps.action:
Action:
1. **Step 1 — Open rule detail page in RAIDEN**
2. Navigate to `/engine/marquee-deprecated` in the Engine Rules Library.
3. Verify rule title, description, WCAG references, and impact (`minor`) are displayed.
4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → marquee-deprecated.
steps.expected_result:
Expected Result:
1. Rule detail page loads without errors.
2. Title and description match engine-rules metadata.
3. WCAG references and remediation advice are visible when present.

payload.title: Unified Engine: menu-avoid — Avoid using role="menu" for web navigation links
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-avoid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-avoid`, `/engine/menu-avoid_success`, `/engine/menu-avoid_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-avoid/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** In most cases, using role=menu on navigation elements within a web page can negatively impact screen reader users, especially those using JAWS. The attribute should be used for menu types that function like those found in desktop applications.
**Remediation advice:** Remove role="menu" from the failing element.
**Fixtures:** 1 pass, 4 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `menu-avoid` examples published at `/engine/menu-avoid_success` and `/engine/menu-avoid_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 1 pass, 4 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: menu-item-avoid — Avoid using role="menuitem" for web navigation links
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-item-avoid`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-item-avoid`, `/engine/menu-item-avoid_success`, `/engine/menu-item-avoid_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-item-avoid/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** In most cases, using ARIA menu roles within a web page can negatively impact screen reader users, especially those using JAWS. role="menuitem" should be used for menu items in menu types that function like those found in desktop applications.
**Remediation advice:** Use standard lists with links and buttons (or custom buttons with role="button") and remove role="menuitem".
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 7 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `menu-item-avoid` examples published at `/engine/menu-item-avoid_success` and `/engine/menu-item-avoid_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 7 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
